Full job description
Company Overview

Smile Materials was built to inspire progress. Guided by joyous, purposeful principles, we transform discarded materials into beautiful, functional architectural panels and surface products. From our factory on the southwest coast of Wales, our work reaches architects, designers and luxury brands around the world. Our distinctive, circular materials are specified for everything from retail displays and counters to furniture, signage and shower panels. Whether a bespoke order or a large-scale production run, we deliver with creativity, precision and care. Together with our network of like-minded manufacturing partners, we’re expanding the reach of circular design globally, turning would-be waste into design with impact.

To support with our extensive growth plans, we have a very exciting opportunity for a multi- talented team member to join our customer facing team. You will be based in London or within commuting distance.

This is a new business sales role, where you’ll have a major impact on our revenue and therefore, we are looking for someone with strong sales/new business generation experience within a relevant industry. You’ll have Sales success within architecture, design, manufacturing or similar and a real passion for what we do and our products. This is essential as you’ll need an appreciation of our market and the right ability and attitude to pick up multiple responsibilities within these areas.

What you will be doing

Develop, create and close lucrative sales opportunities within new and existing customer sectors.
Identify potential targets, ICPs and market opportunities. Qualify thriving market sectors, undiscovered/underperforming opportunities and areas for sales growth/improvement within Smile Materials structures.
Support in presenting strategic proposals for new business sales revenue growth.
Negotiate repeat sales contracts. Deliver excellent sales performance in order to exceed sales targets.
Utilise data and systems to provide monthly and annual sales forecasts of new business growth.
Work with the Head of Sales, production teams and clients to ensure capacity for large contracts are optimised and customer requirements are fulfilled.
Manage the customer journey through our CRM, order book and accounting systems. Compiling quotations, advise on technical details and order confirmations, invoices and activity statements as required.
Pro-actively visit customers.
Stock Management: liaise with the production team to ensure stock is available for sales fulfilment from the order book and sales forecasts.
Who we are looking for:

Educated to Bachelors Degree or equivalent In Business, Marketing or related field
You will have a number of years’ experience in Business Development / New Business Sales within a relevant industry, architecture, interior design, design, plastics manufacturing etc, and a clear interest in our products and materials. Any knowledge or interest in sustainability is also highly desirable
Previous experience of working in a Sales role centred around new business growth within the specification and fit-out sales sector.
Great interpersonal abilities supported by professional oral and written communication skills.
Confidence in building customer relationships face to face, over the phone, email and social media maintaining a positive, patient & friendly rapport in line with our TOV.
The ability to communicate technical detail in a confident and straightforward manner.
Attention to detail and accuracy in all work is important, we make exceptional products, and we like to provide our customers with the best we can.
You must love problem-solving and be proactive taking the initiative to find solutions.
Ability to work to tight timescales and deadlines.
Ideally, we’d love you to have other languages spoken and written.
You must be flexible and adaptable, willing to perform other duties as and when required, we are small and not defined by our job titles.
What we can offer:

Remote working
Experience of working for an innovative, unique, eco-driven company, taking the world’s waste and making it useful again
Competitive market salary and sales incentive bonus scheme
Company share option EMI scheme
Company Pension contribution
Small, friendly Company
Here at Smile Materials, we are all multi-skilled, and as a small, expanding company, we don’t define you by your job title. We encourage our team to develop by supporting knowledge growth, providing in-house and external training.
You may work as Sales Executive, Business Development Manager, Sales Manager, New Business Manager, BDM or similar.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: Up to £57,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Company pension
Flexitime
Work from home
Application question(s):

Are you living or within easy commutable distance from London?
We are looking for candidates with specific industry experience. Do you have experience in working in architecture, interior design, design or manufacturing?
Education:

Bachelor's (preferred)
Experience:

personalisedIntro: I’m applying because this role sits right at the point where I do my best work: opening opportunities, keeping the sales process tight, and making sure delivery holds up behind the promise. My background combines manufacturing, customer-facing operations and performance control, so I’m comfortable working across sales, production and the practical detail that sits between them.

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Commercially minded operator who turns complex delivery into measurable growth.

My background combines manufacturing, customer-facing operations and performance control, so I’m comfortable working across sales, production and the practical detail that sits between them.

I also like that the company is small, multi-skilled and still growing, because that usually means people need to be practical, adaptable and willing to get involved beyond a narrow job title.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This role fits the part of my career where I’ve had to balance commercial pressure with operational discipline. I’d be comfortable building pipeline, qualifying opportunities, keeping forecasts realistic and staying close to customers and production so commitments are properly managed.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership

selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m used to keeping complex work moving without losing sight of detail or deadlines.

selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a customer-facing proof point to show I can protect reputation and relationships.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve turned around customer perception by tightening follow-through and improving the service behind it.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I read this as a role that needs more than sales activity alone. It needs someone who can generate new opportunities, speak credibly about technical products, keep forecasting and CRM discipline in place, and work closely with production so sales growth does not create fulfilment problems.

experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I’m not coming at the role from a purely generic sales background; I’ve worked in a manufacturing environment with growth pressure.

experienceMappings.relevance: The role asks for straightforward communication of technical detail, and I’m used to making complex work understandable and accurate.

experienceMappings.relevance: That’s relevant because customer trust in this role will depend on responsiveness, accuracy and keeping promises.

focusAreasToBring.title: Pipeline discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I’d keep a close eye on opportunity quality, forecast realism and next actions so the sales pipeline is active but controlled.

focusAreasToBring.title: Technical clarity

focusAreasToBring.title: Sales and fulfilment alignment

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background gives me a practical mix of commercial awareness, operational control and customer-facing judgement. I’ve worked in manufacturing, regulated services and live operations, so I’m comfortable with roles where growth depends on both selling well and executing well. That feels close to what Smile Materials needs.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first months building a reliable view of the pipeline, the target sectors and the customer journey through CRM, forecasts and order handling. I’d also focus on learning the product, understanding how production and stock constraints affect sales, and building enough customer confidence to create repeat business rather than one-off wins.
companyHighlights: Smile Materials turns discarded materials into architectural panels and surface products.
The business works with architects, designers and luxury brands.
It operates from southwest Wales and uses manufacturing partners to expand reach.
The company is small, multi-skilled and supports knowledge growth through training.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work well in environments where people need to be flexible, practical and willing to help outside a narrow remit. The small-team, multi-skilled way of working described here suits me.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I’d learn the product range, customer types, sales process, CRM flow and the practical constraints around stock, production and fulfilment.
I’d start sharpening target lists, reviewing live opportunities, and building a clearer rhythm around follow-up, quotations and forecast accuracy.
I’d look to improve conversion on active opportunities, strengthen relationships with key customers and make sure sales activity is aligned with production capacity.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring a steady, commercially aware approach that helps Smile Materials grow new business without losing control of the detail behind it.
closingProofPoints: I’ve improved customer perception in a live service environment.
I’ve worked in specialist manufacturing during a growth phase.
I’ve managed complex, deadline-driven work with strong reporting discipline.
